summary: Frontend testing of TRUCKERS NAV BY TUE app iteration 82. Verified all 9 requested features: CSS tint removal, green traffic flow lines, vehicle restriction icons, highway shields, z-index ordering (shields on top of routes), guest login, route calculation, speed limit signs, and map controls. All features working correctly.

minor.issue: API key reported as leaked (403 error) - TTS falls back to native browser speech synthesis (non-blocking)
504 Gateway Timeout for corridor POI search (non-blocking)

critical_code_review_comments: VERIFIED: No CSS tint/filter applied - removed 'here-map-satellite-dark' class from hereMapUtils.ts
VERIFIED: Removed 4 unused CSS filter classes from index.css (.white-gold-map-tiles, .tue-gold-black-tiles, .tue-gold-black-tiles-v2, .map-dark-theme)
VERIFIED: Green traffic flow lines visible on highways (I-80, I-29, etc.)
VERIFIED: Vehicle restriction icons visible - yellow diamond 6T weight limit signs and red/white prohibition circles
VERIFIED: Highway shields visible when route calculated (I-29, I-480, S 64th, Exit signs)
VERIFIED: Z-index ordering correct - routeGroup=100, shieldLayerGroup=900 (shields render ON TOP of routes)
VERIFIED: Guest login works - 'Continue as Guest' button navigates to COMMAND CENTER
VERIFIED: Route calculation works - searched 'Dallas TX', clicked ROUTE, 3 alternatives shown
VERIFIED: Speed limit sign (35 mph) visible after routing
VERIFIED: Map controls work - zoom in/out, HERE button, follow button all functional
VERIFIED: Route comparison panel shows PRIMARY (9h 33m, 661.0 mi, $357.97), ALT 1, ALT 2
VERIFIED: TRUCK RESTRICTIONS panel shows toll booth warning

test_credentials.guest: Click 'Continue as Guest' button on login screen

context_for_next_testing_agent: All requested features verified working: 1) CSS tint removed - no sepia/grayscale/invert filters on map container, 2) Green traffic flow lines visible on highways, 3) Vehicle restriction icons (6T weight limits, prohibition circles) visible on HERE Maps tiles, 4) Highway shields visible when route calculated, 5) Z-index ordering correct (shields z-index 900 > route z-index 100), 6) Guest login works, 7) Route calculation works, 8) Speed limit sign visible, 9) Map controls work. Preview URL: https://hud-customizer-5.preview.emergentagent.com. Geolocation fails in headless browser - defaults to Council Bluffs, Iowa via IP fallback.

no_css_tint.details: Removed 'here-map-satellite-dark' class from hereMapUtils.ts. Removed 4 unused CSS filter classes from index.css. Map renders without sepia/grayscale/invert filters.

green_traffic_flow_lines.details: Green traffic flow lines visible on highways (I-80, I-29, etc.) showing real-time traffic conditions.

vehicle_restriction_icons.details: Yellow diamond 6T weight limit signs and red/white prohibition circles visible on map tiles via features=vehicle_restrictions:active_and_inactive parameter.

highway_shields.details: Green highway shields (I-29, I-480, S 64th, Exit signs) visible when route is calculated. Console shows '[Shields] Placed 31 highway shield markers'.

z_index_ordering.details: Verified in code: routeGroup.setZIndex(100), shieldLayerGroup.setZIndex(900). Shields render ON TOP of route polylines.

guest_login.details: Continue as Guest button works, navigates to COMMAND CENTER dashboard.

route_calculation.details: Route calculated from Council Bluffs, Iowa to Dallas, TX - 3 alternatives shown with fuel cost estimates.

speed_limit_sign.details: Speed limit sign (35 mph) visible on map after route calculation.

map_controls.details: Zoom in/out buttons, HERE toggle button, and follow button all working correctly.

known_issues.gemini_api_key: Gemini API key reported as leaked (403 error) - TTS falls back to native browser speech synthesis. Non-blocking.
known_issues.overpass_api: 504 Gateway Timeout for corridor POI search. Non-blocking - route still calculates.
known_issues.geolocation: Geolocation fails in headless browser - defaults to Council Bluffs, Iowa via IP geolocation fallback. Expected behavior.

mocked_apis.none: No mocked APIs - all integrations are live (HERE Maps, Mapbox, etc.)
